data.title: Domino's Pizza slips after missing Q3 sales estimates
data.description:
Domino's Pizza (NYSE:DPZ) slipped in early trading after falling slightly short of revenue estimates with its Q3 earnings report and setting cautious guidance.

The pizza chain operator reported revenue increased 4.9% to $1.08 billion in the quarter that ended on September 8, primarily due to higher supply chain, U.S. franchise advertising and U.S. franchise royalties and fees revenues. However, that tally was short of the consensus estimate by $20 million. An increase in supply chain revenues was primarily attributable to increased order volume, as well as an increase in the company's food basket pricing to stores, but was partially offset by a shift in the relative mix of the products sold by the company. In terms of pricing, Domino's (DPZ) food basket pricing was up 1.3% compared to a year ago.

Domestic company-owned comparable store sales growth was +3.1% during the quarter, vs. +3.5% consensus.

U.S. company-owned store gross margin increased 1.0 percentage point in the quarter, primarily driven by sales leverage due to higher customer transaction counts. Supply chain gross margin increased 0.6 percentage points in the quarter, primarily due to procurement productivity. EPS was reported at $4.19 vs. $3.64 consensus and $4.18 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Domino's Pizza (DPZ) expects approximately 6% annual global retail sales growth in 2024, approximately 8% annual income from operations growth, and global net store growth of 800 to 850. For 2025, the company expects annual global retail sales growth and annual income from operations growth to be generally in line with its 2024 expectations. "The Hungry for MORE pillar of Renowned Value will be the primary focus for our business in the near-term as we look to continue to create our own tailwinds around the world," highlighted CEO Russell Weiner.

Shares of Domino's Pizza (DPZ) were down 0.40% in premarket action on Thursday.

data.title: Empire Petroleum extends expiration of subscription rights offering
data.description:
• Empire Petroleum (NYSE:EP) said it has extended the expiration date of its subscription rights offering, under which it expects to raise ~$10M.
  • The extension is mainly to let stockholders have more time to consider their participation and arrange finances for the rights offering.
  • The rights offering is now expected to expire at 5:00 p.m ET, on October 24.

data.title: Riyadh Air collaborates with Sabre for offer optimization
data.description:
• Riyadh Air entered a strategic agreement with Sabre (NASDAQ:SABR) under which Riyadh Air will adopt comprehensive SabreMosaic Offer Optimization technology.
  • This technology enables Riyadh Air to offer diverse and customized travel options, delivering tailored experiences to customers while optimizing revenue.
  • "This collaboration ensures we are equipped to meet the evolving needs of our customers and to deliver a truly differentiated experience. Sabre's intelligent solutions act as the catalyst for our offer and order platform, and we are excited to continue working together to achieve our ambitious goals." said Vincent Coste, Chief Commercial Officer, Riyadh Air.

data.title: Spotify begins free streaming option in South Korea - report
data.description:
Spotify (NYSE:SPOT) said its free music and podcasts service option is now available in South Korea, Reuters reported.

The Luxembourg-based company's freemium business model offers a free, limited ad-supported service and an unlimited premium subscription service, the report added.

The move will help the company to better connect the global audience with Korean music, including K-pop, Spotify noted.

Users in South Korea would be able to sign-up for a free account to access Spotify's playlists, podcasts, and daily mix among other things and can also opt to upgrade to premium on-demand, ad-free music, the report noted.

Spotify launched in South Korea in 2021 and since then the average monthly streams of artists from the country on the platform is now over 5.8B streams, according to the company.

The company added that brands which advertise on free tier can deliver their message effectively, as Spotify ads capture twice as much attention than social media platforms globally.

data.title: Atlas Energy Solutions provides Q3 update
data.description:
• Atlas Energy Solutions (NYSE:AESI) expects higher plant operating expenses to cause Q3 operating results to fall below prior guidance.
  • The company now expects Q3 revenue to be between $300M and $310M with adjusted EBITDA of $70M to $75M.
  • Proppant sales volumes are expected to be ~6M tons.
  • The company also expects to book an asset write-down of approximately $9M related to one of its dredge mining assets.
  • “Following the rebuild of the feed system at our Kermit facility that was completed in July, our focus has turned to the commissioning of the Dune Express and ensuring that our mining operations are optimized to produce the increased volumes we expect to provide our customers in the Delaware Basin next year." stated John Turner, President and CEO.

data.title: HWH International files to sell 5M shares of common stock, warrants
data.description:
• HWH International (NASDAQ:HWH) filed a prospectus related to the sale of 5M shares.
  • The prospectus is also related to the offering of warrants to purchase up to 5M shares.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Analysts see sustained rally in gold, ING forecasts Brent to avg $72 over 2025
data.description:
Gold prices extended their gains to a fifth session on Thursday as investors positioned for the key economic data, while oil and gas prices have moved higher following an Iranian missile attack on Israel.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) +0.33% to $2,616.97, although futures stuck to a tight range, as the U.S. dollar held firm and reduced expectations for a larger rate cut in November from the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Analysts at BMI, nevertheless, upped their 2024 gold price forecast from $2,250/oz to $2,375/oz, expecting prices to trade within the range of $2,500/oz-2,800 in the coming months.

This marks a departure from the $1,943/oz average seen in 2023, with the brokerage now neutral to bullish towards gold for Q4 2024 - Q1 2025 amid high levels of geopolitical tension.

That said, BMI noted some downside risks to gold, especially if Donald Trump wins the U.S. elections in December, which would support the U.S. dollar and pressure gold.

On the energy side, markets are waiting to see how Israel responds. Escalation in the region is putting energy supply at greater risk, analysts at ING said.

Crude oil (CL1:COM) rose +1.56% to $74.38, while Brent (CO1:COM) climbed +1.52% to $77.74.

And with the global market set to be in surplus through 2025, this means that oil prices will likely weaken, with ING forecasting that ICE Brent will average $72/bbl over 2025.

Also, front-month November Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) eased 0.87% to $2.64, after finishing 2.6% lower at $2.660/MMBtu, with analysts pointing to anticipated demand destruction from Hurricane Milton, weaker ERCOT demand, and the potential for eventually stronger U.S. production.

Elsewhere, in an interesting turn of events, a push into metals trading by several giants of the energy markets has reportedly spurred a hiring spree and driven up salaries, with some senior executives saying they’ve never seen such a frenzied competition.

Some of the biggest oil and gas traders are looking to put part of the mega profits from the last few years into metals businesses, amid forecasts for rapid demand growth due to the energy transition.

At Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF), Vitol has attempted to hire key members of the iron ore team including department head Peter Hill, Bloomberg reported. Mercuria’s metals team now stands at about 60 people, many of them alumni of Trafigura, but also Glencore, IXM and Goldman Sachs Group.

Recent Commodity Price Movements and A look At Some ETFs

  • Energy

      • Crude oil (CL1:COM) +1.50% to $74.34
      • Natural Gas (NG1:COM) -0.68% to $2.64

data.title: Domino's Pizza declares $1.51 dividend
data.description:
• Domino's Pizza (NYSE:DPZ) declares $1.51/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.46%
  • Payable Dec. 27; for shareholders of record Dec. 13; ex-div Dec. 13.
  • See DPZ Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Deutsche Telekom plans €2 billion buyback, gives long-term guidance
data.description:
Deutsche Telekom AG (OTCQX:DTEGY) plans a share buyback program of €2 billion in 2025 and proposes a dividend of 90 cents per share for the 2024 financial year, up from 77 cents last year.

The German telecommunications company plans to lean on artificial intelligence and a data-driven business model to make itself more efficient over the coming years.

The group expects average annual growth of around 4% in both net revenue and service revenues through 2027.

Adjusted EPS is expected to rise by an average of more than 11% per year, reaching around €2.50 in 2027. Free cash flow AL is expected to increase to around €21 billion by that time.

“We are initiating the next stage,” said Tim Höttges, CEO of Deutsche Telekom. By 2027, Deutsche Telekom plans to generate a total of more than €15 billion, on top of the investments in the business and the dividend payments.

Deutsche Telekom (OTCQX:DTEGY) has the financial flexibility to deploy higher returns, mainly due to the growing subscriber base at T-Mobile US (TMUS) where it has a majority stake.

data.title: Domino's Pizza GAAP EPS of $4.19 beats by $0.55, revenue of $1.08B misses by $20M
data.description:
• Domino's Pizza press release (NYSE:DPZ): Q3 GAAP EPS of $4.19 beats by $0.55.
  • Revenue of $1.08B (+4.9% Y/Y) misses by $20M.

  • Global retail sales growth (excluding foreign currency impact) of 5.1%

  • U.S. same store sales growth of 3.0%

  • International same store sales growth (excluding foreign currency impact) of 0.8%

  • Global net store growth of 72

  • The Company now expects the following given the challenging macroeconomic environment and its impact on current business trends across the globe:

    • Approximately 6% annual global retail sales growth.
    • Approximately 8% annual income from operations growth.
    • Global net store growth of 800 to 850.

  • 2025 Guidance

    • The Company expects annual global retail sales growth and annual income from operations growth to be generally in line with its 2024 expectations.
